Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: unify environment and environment_values into single config (#369)

* fix: unify environment and environment_values into single config

The separate `environment` (passthrough keys) and `environment_values`
(KEY=VALUE pairs) settings were confusing and led to silent failures
when entries were placed in the wrong list. Merge them into a single
`environment: Vec<String>` where bare `KEY` passes through from host
and `KEY=VALUE` sets explicitly (with `$HOST_VAR` and `$$` escaping).

Also makes inherited settings always expanded in the sandbox config
popup since it's read-only informational content.

- Add v004 migration to merge environment_values into environment array
- Replace extra_env_keys/extra_env_values with unified extra_env field
- Merge two TUI env lists into one accepting both formats
- Remove EnvironmentValues from settings TUI
- Add comprehensive tests for collect_environment()

Fixes #368

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update documentation to reflect unified environment config

Remove all references to the old `environment_values` field from both
docs/ and website/ guides. Update examples and tables to show the
unified `environment` list format (bare KEY or KEY=VALUE).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: Session 17 — pre-frontend backend audit (12 fixes)

Bugs fixed:
- soft_hedge_router now overwrites verified_response (silence mechanism was broken)
- Ingest route strips +psycopg from DATABASE_URL (HTTP ingestion was failing)
- mirror_only generation uses temperature=0.0 (was 0.7, non-deterministic)

Security fixes:
- SQL injection in provenance.py: f-string interpolation → parameterized ANY(%s)
- Path traversal in file upload: sanitize filename to basename only
- PATCH /review scoped to clone: prevents cross-tenant review manipulation
- WebSocket DB session leak: removed unused Depends(get_db) parameter
- user_memory removed from API response: prevents personal data exposure

Code quality:
- _psycopg_url() extracted to shared core/db/__init__.py (was duplicated)
- _naive_split uses regex split (was appending double periods)
- BackgroundTasks: removed mutable default argument
- requirements.txt: removed orphaned tf-keras, pinned pymupdf/mem0ai/edge-tts

All 69 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- aads.service: systemd oneshot docker compose up/down

commit.message:
fix: remove beige backgrounds from email — switch to clean white

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(scroll): add inverted prop (#2131)

* feat(scroll): add inverted prop

Re-introduce the inverted prop for FlashList v2. This uses platform-specific
transforms (scaleY on iOS/web, rotate on Android for performance) applied at
the container, cell, header, and footer levels.

Includes a ChatInverted fixture example and e2e test.

Fixes #1844

* fix(e2e): make ChatInverted sample deterministic and add reference screenshots

Remove timestamp display and use index-based message generation to ensure
consistent snapshots across e2e test runs.

* fix: scroll examples list in Twitter e2e test before tapping FlatList item

The new Chat (Inverted) entry pushed Twitter FlatList Timeline just
off-screen on iPhone 16 Pro, causing the e2e test to fail with
"View is not hittable". Add a swipe before tapping that item.

* fix: apply inverted counter-transform to empty component, sticky headers, and backdrop

These were rendered inside the inverted container without
counter-transforms, causing them to appear upside-down.

The invertedTransformStyle is undefined when inverted is falsy,
so these changes are no-ops for non-inverted lists.

* refactor: centralize inverted transform style and add ListEmptyComponentStyle

- Extract duplicated inverted transform ternary into getInvertedTransformStyle utility
- Replace duplicated logic in RecyclerView, ViewHolder, and useSecondaryProps
- Add ListEmptyComponentStyle prop matching ListHeader/FooterComponentStyle pattern
- Only wrap ListEmptyComponent in CompatView when inverted or styled (no regression for non-inverted)
- Add InvertedTest fixture sample with items/empty/sticky toggle scenarios
- Add InvertedTest e2e tests with reference screenshots
- Add ListEmptyComponentStyle to docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move inverted guard from utility to call sites

* docs: add inverted prop to usage documentation

* fix(e2e): scroll examples list before tapping Twitter Timeline

The new inverted samples pushed Twitter Timeline below the visible
area on the examples screen, causing all Twitter e2e tests to fail
with "View is not hittable".

---------

commit.message:
Fix shelter rest exit to place player at the open front end

Exit offset was using positive Z which placed the player at the closed
back end (where the tarp meets the ground). Flipped to negative Z and
rotated player facing 180 degrees so they exit at the open front.

fix: explicitly set buildCommand to next build (force webpack, no Turbopack)

Vercel auto-enables Turbopack for Next.js 15+ which requires the lightningcss
native binary. Overriding buildCommand to plain next build uses webpack which
has no native binary dependency.

feat: auto-seed missing categories on startup without disk reset

Partial DB (new categories) → seed_category_all_cities() per gap

commit.message:
Phase 6: Use email summaries in GLM-5 final answer generation

Update GLM-5 orchestrator to prioritize summaries:
- Display summaries FIRST with key points and action items
- Include email IDs for reference in final answers
- Add requested emails section for on-demand fetched content
- Reduce full email limit from 15 to 10 (summaries provide broader context)
- Increase body truncation from 2000 to 3000 chars (reading fewer emails)

Context hierarchy:
1. Email summaries (AI-generated with key points)
2. Requested emails (full content on demand)
3. Auto-read emails (fallback)
4. Search results (fallback)

commit.message:
fix(ci): set GOOGLE_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET to fix API env validation

The API's env validation requires all 4 GOOGLE_OIDC_* vars to be
either all empty or all non-empty. CI had 3 of 4 set (CLIENT_SECRET
was empty), causing the API server to crash before serving health
checks — resulting in silent 40s timeout failures.

Add time slot management for event panels (#161)

Introduce full CRUD for time slots in the organizer panel, allowing
event organizers to create, edit, and delete time slots within event
date boundaries. Time slots are validated against overlaps and event
bounds. A new TimeSlotRequirement model links time slots to proposal
categories (required/optional), mirroring the existing session field
requirements pattern.

Key changes:
- TimeSlotRepository with create/read/update/delete/list_by_event
- TimeSlotRequirement model and migration for per-category configuration
- Panel views: list (paginated by day), create, edit, delete
- PanelService.validate_time_slot for overlap and bounds checking
- CFP edit page extended with time slot requirement management
- Integration tests for all new views and the CFP edit time slot flow

CPL V2: Full HYDRA integration with 7 hard gates

BREAKING CHANGES:
- _check_entry_quality() completely rewritten
- All data failures now REJECT (no more default pass)
- SPY block removed (V7 disabled)

NEW GATES (ALL must pass):
1. Polygon health check
2. HYDRA connection required
3. Direction alignment (BULLISH=calls, BEARISH=puts, NEUTRAL=reject)
4. Regime check (CHOPPY/UNKNOWN=reject)
5. Blowup probability (<70% required)
6. GEX flip proximity (>1% from flip required)
7. Volume confirmation (1.2x ratio required)

CONFIDENCE SCORING:
- Base 55% when all gates pass
- +5-10 flow bias alignment
- +10 negative GEX (trending)
- +5 sweep direction
- +0-10 volume strength
- +0-10 momentum strength
- -20 penalty for elevated blowup

ADDED FILES:
- ops/COMPACT_SUMMARY.md - Quick deployment reference
- ops/HANDOFF.md - Full context for next session
- wsb_snake/utils/polygon_health.py - API health monitoring
- dashboard/api/routes/health.py - Health endpoint

FIXES:
- March 3 failure root cause: CPL ignored HYDRA
- Position sizing verified: $2,500 capital, 1 trade max
- Session halt: 1:00 PM ET

Replace the old inline copy button in the embed panel with PluginPostStatusInfo rows that show both the embed HTML and the shortcode, each using CopyCodeRow. Files added: copy-code-row.tsx, copy-code-row.scss; modified: embed-code-panel.tsx to use CopyCodeRow, to generate embedCode and shortcode, and to switch from PluginDocumentSettingPanel to PluginPostStatusInfo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

deployments: remove model-specific encryption handlers section (#2923)

Remove the "Model-specific handlers" subsection from the encryption
at-rest docs

## Why

The `@encryption.encrypt.json.assistant` /
`@encryption.encrypt.json.run` sub-decorator pattern shown in this
section does not exist in the `langgraph_sdk` `Encryption` class. The
class only exposes `encrypt.blob`, `encrypt.json`, `decrypt.blob`,
`decrypt.json`, and `context` decorators — no further chaining to model
types is supported.

These model-specific annotations were removed from the SDK a while ago,
but I must've forgotten to update the docs or lost a commit at the end
of the long-lived PR that added this doc.

Verified by reading the installed SDK source at
`langgraph_sdk/encryption/__init__.py`.

## Review notes

Just a deletion — no new content, no other files changed.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Claude/document news hub architecture 3 xi12 (#295)

* feat: add Related News tab to regulation detail pages

Closes the gap where news articles linked to a regulation were not
surfaced on the regulation detail page.

- server/routers/hub.ts: add `getNewsByRegulation` public endpoint
  that filters hub_news by JSON_CONTAINS on relatedRegulationIds
- HubRegulationDetail.tsx: add 6th "News" tab showing NewsCardCompact
  cards for all articles linked to the regulation, with a count badge
  and an empty state when no articles exist yet

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EhzKvTkBpGo5zZem3byqpX

* feat(news-impact): add advisory staleness and regulation verification flags (E-01, E-02)

- Add drizzle migration 0023: stale_since on advisory_reports, needs_verification on regulations
- Update schema.ts to reflect new columns
- Add server/services/news-impact/index.ts with flagAdvisoryReportsStaleSince,
  flagRegulationsNeedVerification, and isVerificationTriggerState helpers
- Hook both functions into createHubNews() in server/db.ts (fire-and-forget after insert)
- Add unit tests in server/routers/__tests__/news-impact.test.ts

This closes the NEWS_HUB → ADVISORY and NEWS_HUB → CATALOG feedback loops:
advisory reports are now marked stale when a related regulation receives news
coverage, and regulations are flagged for re-verification when enforcement or
delegated-act signals are detected.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EhzKvTkBpGo5zZem3byqpX

* feat(esrs-mapping): add effective confidence decay for stale source material (E-03)

- Add computeEffectiveConfidence() and applyDecayToRows() helpers to
  server/db-esrs-gs1-mapping.ts
- Decay rules: HIGH→MEDIUM at 90 days, additional downgrade at 180 days,
  and an additional decay step when regulation_needs_verification = 1
- Apply decay to getAllEsrsGs1Mappings, getEsrsGs1MappingsByStandard, and
  getGs1AttributesForEsrsMapping; each row now carries effectiveConfidence
  and decayReason alongside the original confidence value
- getEsrsGs1MappingsByStandard and getGs1AttributesForEsrsMapping join
  regulations to check needs_verification flag

Confidence scores that silently remain HIGH on months-old, unverified source
material are now surfaced as MEDIUM with an explicit decay reason, making
advisory artifacts more trustworthy over time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EhzKvTkBpGo5zZem3byqpX

* feat(ask-isa): add intent routing, inline ESRS recommendations, and news retrieval (E-04, E-05, E-06)

E-04 — classifyQueryIntent(): rule-based regex classifier prepended to
askEnhanced. Classifies REGULATORY_CHANGE, GAP_ANALYSIS, ESRS_MAPPING,
NEWS_QUERY, and GENERAL_QA. Intent is returned on every askEnhanced response.

E-05 — buildInlineRecommendations(): after LLM answer generation, extracts
ESRS standard codes (regex /ESRS [ESAG]\d+/gi), fetches top-3 GS1 attribute
mappings per standard from getEsrsGs1MappingsByStandard, and appends
inlineRecommendations[] to the response. Transforms Q&A sessions from
informational to immediately actionable.

E-06 — searchRecentNewsArticles(): for REGULATORY_CHANGE and NEWS_QUERY
intents, queries hub_news (last 30 days, credibilityScore >= 0.7) using
keyword matching and merges results into the retrieval set before LLM context
building. News results are formatted as [Recent News - {date}] in the LLM
prompt for temporal grounding.

Also includes unit tests for the intent classifier and ESRS code extraction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EhzKvTkBpGo5zZem3byqpX
